item.title: 什么是 NodeList，它们是如何工作的？ | What are NodeLists, and how do they work?
item.description:
您是否知道 Javascript 不会将多个元素的选择分类为数组？ 相反，它是一种称为NodeList的东西。 节点列表本质上是元素列表。 要生成NodeList ，我们可以这样做：

 let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' );

上面的代码将返回页面上所有段落的列表作为NodeList 。

节点列表很有趣，因为它们不是数组，因此它们不会继承数组具有的所有不同功能。 一个值得注意的例子是，在一些较旧的浏览器中，例如 Internet Explorer， NodeList不继承forEach函数。

因此，如果您想为每个段落添加事件侦听器，以下代码将在 Internet Explorer 中引发错误：

 let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' ); myNodeList . forEach ( function ( item ) { item . addEventListener ( ' click ' , function ( e ) { // Do some click events }); // For each node item.. });

由于这适用于大多数现代浏览器，您通常不必担心使用它，但如果您想支持旧浏览器并使用forEach ，我们必须将 NodeList 放入数组中，如下所示：

要将我们的 NodeList 转换为更易于管理的数组，我们可以使用以下代码：

 let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' ); Array . prototype . forEach . call ( myNodeList , function ( item ) { item . addEventListener ( ' click ' , function ( e ) { // Do some click events }); // For each node item.. });

有点复杂，但现在我们可以确保所有用户都可以访问我们添加到 NodeList 项中的事件侦听器。

由于本文到目前为止一直关注NodeList并非总是默认具有forEach ，因此您可能想知道可以在 NodeList 上运行哪些函数。 有4个：

  • 值得注意的是NodeList.item是 Internet Explorer 唯一支持的函数。 其余的都没有。

    例如，下面是我们如何在 NodeList 上运行这些函数：

    节点列表条目

    let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' ); // entries let allEntries = myNodeList . entries (); for ( var i of allEntries ) { // Console logs each paragraph with an id individually, such as [ 0, p ] [ 1, p ] [ 2, p ] ... console . log ( i ); }

    节点列表.forEach

     let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' ); // forEach - iterate over each item myNodeList . forEach ( function ( item ) { // Console logs each paragraph element individually console . log ( i ); });

    节点列表项

    let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' ); // item - get the first element (0) let firstElement = myNodeList . item ( 0 ); // Console logs the first element only console . log ( firstElement );

    节点列表键

    let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' ); let getKeys = myNodeList . keys (); // Console logs the id of each element, ie 1 2 3 4 5 ... for ( var i of getKeys ) { console . log ( i ); }

    节点列表.values

     let myNodeList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' p ' ); let getValues = myNodeList . values (); // Console logs each HTML element as an array, ie pppp ... for ( var i of getValues ) { console . log ( i ); }

一旦暂停，smpnjn 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，smpnjn 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，smpnjn 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 smpnjn 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 smpnjn 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 smpnjn 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Did you know that Javascript does not class a selection of multiple elements as an array? Instead, it is something called a NodeList. A node list is essentially a list of elements. To generate a NodeList, we can do something like this:

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p');

The above code will return a list of all paragraphs found on a page as a NodeList.

Node lists are interesting because they aren't arrays, so they do not inherit all of the different functions that arrays have. One notable example is that, in some older browsers, such as Internet Explorer, NodeLists do not inherit the forEach function.

As such, if you wanted to add an event listener to every paragraph, the following code would throw an error in Internet Explorer:

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p'); myNodeList.forEach(function(item) { item.addEventListener('click', function(e) { // Do some click events }); // For each node item..
});

Since this works in most modern browsers, you usually don't have to worry about using this, but if you want to support older browsers and use forEach, we have to throw our NodeList into an array, like so:

To convert our NodeList into a more manageable array, we can use the following code:

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p');
Array.prototype.forEach.call(myNodeList, function(item) { item.addEventListener('click', function(e) { // Do some click events }); // For each node item..
});

A little complicated, but now we can ensure all our users can access the event listeners we add to our NodeList items.

What functions do NodeLists support?

Since this article has focused so far on how NodeLists haven't always had forEach by default, you may be wondering what functions can be run on a NodeList. There are 4:

  • NodeList.entries - returns an iterator for getting both the id and element as an id/element pair, i.e. [ 1, p ].
  • NodeList.forEach - for iterating through each item individually.
  • NodeList.item - for getting a specific item by id, i.e. get the first paragraph by NodeList.item(0).
  • NodeList.keys - returns an iterator for getting keys, i.e. 1 2 3 4 5 ...
  • NodeList.values - returns an iterator for getting the HTML elements, i.e. p p p p ...

It is worth noting that NodeList.item is the only function which is supported by Internet Explorer. The rest are not.

As an example, here is how we would run these functions on our NodeList:

NodeList.entries 

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p'); // entries
let allEntries = myNodeList.entries();
for(var i of allEntries) { // Console logs each paragraph with an id individually, such as [ 0, p ] [ 1, p ] [ 2, p ] ... console.log(i);
}

NodeList.forEach 

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p'); // forEach - iterate over each item
myNodeList.forEach(function(item) { // Console logs each paragraph element individually console.log(i);
});

NodeList.item 

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p'); // item - get the first element (0)
let firstElement = myNodeList.item(0);
// Console logs the first element only
console.log(firstElement);

NodeList.keys 

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p'); let getKeys = myNodeList.keys();
// Console logs the id of each element, i.e. 1 2 3 4 5 ...
for(var i of getKeys) { console.log(i);
}

NodeList.values 

let myNodeList = document.querySelectorAll('p'); let getValues = myNodeList.values();
// Console logs each HTML element as an array, i.e. p p p p ... for(var i of getValues) { console.log(i);
}

Once suspended, smpnjn will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, smpnjn will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by smpnjn will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If smpnjn is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag smpnjn:

smpnjn consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging smpnjn will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: smpnjn

item.title: 性能比较：减少存储与 Minio | Performance comparison: Reduct Storage vs. Minio
item.description:
我们经常使用像 S3 这样的 blob 存储，如果我们需要将不同格式和大小的数据存储在云中的某个地方或我们的内部存储中。 您还可以对其进行调整以将历史数据保留为 blob 的时间序列。 最直接的解决方案是为每个数据源创建一个文件夹并保存名称中带有时间戳的对象：

 bucket | |---cv_camera |---1666225094312397.bin |---1666225094412397.bin |---1666225094512397.bin

如果您需要查询数据，您应该请求cv_camera文件夹中的对象列表，并使用给定时间间隔内的名称过滤它们。
这种方法实现起来很简单，但也有一些缺点：

  • 文件夹中的对象越多，查询的时间就越长。
  • 小对象的大开销：时间戳作为字符串，由于文件系统的块大小，最小文件大小为 1Kb 或 512
  • 减少存储旨在解决这些问题。 它具有强大的 FIFO 配额、用于通过时间间隔查询数据的 HTTP API，并将对象（或记录）组合成块以实现高效的磁盘使用和搜索。

    用 Minio 读/写数据

    对于基准测试，我们创建了两个函数来写入和读取CHUNK_COUNT块：

     from minio import Minio import time minio_client = Minio ( "127.0.0.1:9000" , access_key = "minioadmin" , secret_key = "minioadmin" , secure = False ) def write_to_minio (): count = 0 for i in range ( CHUNK_COUNT ): count += CHUNK_SIZE object_name = f "data/ { str ( int ( time . time_ns () / 1000 )) } .bin" minio_client . put_object ( BUCKET_NAME , object_name , io . BytesIO ( CHUNK ), CHUNK_SIZE ) return count # count data to print it in main function def read_from_minio ( t1 , t2 ): count = 0 t1 = str ( int ( t1 * 1000_000 )) t2 = str ( int ( t2 * 1000_000 )) for obj in minio_client . list_objects ( "test" , prefix = "data/" ): if t1 <= obj . object_name [ 5 : - 4 ] <= t2 : resp = minio_client . get_object ( "test" , obj . object_name ) count += len ( resp . read ()) return count

    您可以认为minio_client不提供任何带有模式的 API 查询数据，因此我们必须在客户端浏览整个文件夹以找到所需的对象。 如果你有数十亿个对象，它就会停止工作。 您应该将对象路径存储在某个时间序列数据库中或创建文件夹层次结构，例如，每天创建一个文件夹。

    使用减少存储读取/写入数据

    使用减少存储，这是一种更容易的方式：

     from reduct import Client as ReductClient reduct_client = ReductClient ( "http://127.0.0.1:8383" ) async def write_to_reduct (): count = 0 bucket = await reduct_client . create_bucket ( "test" , exist_ok = True ) for i in range ( CHUNK_COUNT ): await bucket . write ( "data" , CHUNK ) count += CHUNK_SIZE return count async def read_from_reduct ( t1 , t2 ): count = 0 bucket = await reduct_client . get_bucket ( "test" ) async for rec in bucket . query ( "data" , int ( t1 * 1000000 ), int ( t2 * 1000000 )): count += len ( await rec . read_all ()) return count

    基准

    当我们拥有写/读功能时，我们终于可以编写我们的基准测试了：

     import io import random import time import asyncio from minio import Minio from reduct import Client as ReductClient CHUNK_SIZE = 100000 CHUNK_COUNT = 10000 BUCKET_NAME = "test" CHUNK = random . randbytes ( CHUNK_SIZE ) minio_client = Minio ( "127.0.0.1:9000" , access_key = "minioadmin" , secret_key = "minioadmin" , secure = False ) reduct_client = ReductClient ( "http://127.0.0.1:8383" ) # Our function were here.. if __name__ == "__main__" : print ( f "Chunk size= { CHUNK_SIZE / 1000_000 } Mb, count= { CHUNK_COUNT } " ) ts = time . time () size = write_to_minio () print ( f "Write { size / 1000_000 } Mb to Minio: { time . time () - ts } s" ) ts_read = time . time () size = read_from_minio ( ts , time . time ()) print ( f "Read { size / 1000_000 } Mb from Minio: { time . time () - ts_read } s" ) loop = asyncio . new_event_loop (); ts = time . time () size = loop . run_until_complete ( write_to_reduct ()) print ( f "Write { size / 1000_000 } Mb to Reduct Storage: { time . time () - ts } s" ) ts_read = time . time () size = loop . run_until_complete ( read_from_reduct ( ts , time . time ())) print ( f "Read { size / 1000_000 } Mb from Reduct Storage: { time . time () - ts_read } s" )

    对于测试，我们需要运行数据库。 使用 docker-compose 很容易做到：

     services : reduct-storage : image : reductstorage/engine:v1.0.1 volumes : - ./reduct-data:/data ports : - 8383:8383 minio : image : minio/minio volumes : - ./minio-data:/data command : minio server /data --console-address :9002 ports : - 9000:9000 - 9002:9002

    运行 docker compose 配置和基准测试：

     docker-compose up -d python3 main.py

    结果

    该脚本打印给定 CHUNK_SIZE 和CHUNK_COUNT的结果。 在我的设备上，我得到了以下数字：

    块手术米尼奥减少存储
    写读写读写读如您所见， Reduce Storage对于写入操作总是更快（对于 10 Mb blob 是 16 倍！！！），而当我们有许多小对象时，读取操作会慢一些。 您可能会注意到，当我们减小块的大小时，两个数据库的速度都会降低。 这可以用 HTTP 开销来解释，因为我们为每个写入或读取操作花费一个专用的 HTTP 请求。

    结论

    减少存储可能是应用程序的一个不错的选择，在这些应用程序中，您应该使用时间戳来存储 blob 的历史记录并始终写入数据。 它具有强大的 FIFO 配额以避免磁盘空间问题，并且对于密集的写入操作非常快。

    参考：

    这是为期一个月的开源庆祝活动。 对于许多开发人员来说，这是他们对开源的介绍。

    查看 DEV 上的 Hacktoberfest 标签以了解最新消息！

    一旦暂停，reduc-storage 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

    一旦解除暂停，reduc-storage 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，reduc-storage 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果减少存储没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记减少存储的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记减少存储将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    We often use blob storage like S3, If we need to store data of different formats and sizes somewhere in the cloud or in our internal storage. Minio is a S3 compatible storage which you can run on your private cloud, bare-metal server or even on an edge device. You can also adapt it to keep historical data as time series of blobs. The most straightforward solution would be to create a folder for each data source and save objects with timestamps in their names:

    bucket
 |
 |---cv_camera
        |---1666225094312397.bin
        |---1666225094412397.bin
        |---1666225094512397.bin

    If you need to query data, you should request a list of objects in the cv_camera folder and filter them with names which are in the given time interval.
    This approach is simple for implementation, but it has some disadvantages:

    • the more objects the folder has, the longer the querying is.
    • big overhead for small objects: timestamps as strings and minimal file size is 1Kb or 512 due to the block size of the file system
    • FIFO quota, to remove old data when we reach some limit, may not work for intensive write operations.

    Reduct Storage aims to solve these issues. It has a strong FIFO quota, an HTTP API for querying data via time intervals, and it composes objects (or records) into blocks for an efficient disk usage and search.

    Minio and Reduct Storage have Python SDKs, so we can use them for implementation write and read operations and compare the performance.

    Read/Write Data With Minio

    For benchmarks, we create two functions to write and read CHUNK_COUNT chunks:

    from minio import Minio
import time minio_client = Minio("127.0.0.1:9000", access_key="minioadmin", secret_key="minioadmin", secure=False) def write_to_minio(): count = 0 for i in range(CHUNK_COUNT): count += CHUNK_SIZE object_name = f"data/{str(int(time.time_ns() / 1000))}.bin" minio_client.put_object(BUCKET_NAME, object_name, io.BytesIO(CHUNK), CHUNK_SIZE) return count # count data to print it in main function
 def read_from_minio(t1, t2): count = 0 t1 = str(int(t1 * 1000_000)) t2 = str(int(t2 * 1000_000)) for obj in minio_client.list_objects("test", prefix="data/"): if t1 <= obj.object_name[5:-4] <= t2: resp = minio_client.get_object("test", obj.object_name) count += len(resp.read()) return count

    You can that minio_client doesn't provide any API query data with patterns, so we have to browse the whole folder on the client side to find the needed object. If you have billions of objects, it stops working. You should store object paths in some time series database or create a hierarchy of folders, e.g., create a folder per day.

    Read/Write Data With Reduct Storage

    With Reduct Storage this is a way easier:

    from reduct import Client as ReductClient
reduct_client = ReductClient("http://127.0.0.1:8383") async def write_to_reduct(): count = 0 bucket = await reduct_client.create_bucket("test", exist_ok=True) for i in range(CHUNK_COUNT): await bucket.write("data", CHUNK) count += CHUNK_SIZE return count async def read_from_reduct(t1, t2): count = 0 bucket = await reduct_client.get_bucket("test") async for rec in bucket.query("data", int(t1 * 1000000), int(t2 * 1000000)): count += len(await rec.read_all()) return count

    Benchmarks

    When we have the write/read functions, we can finally write our benchmarks:

    import io
import random
import time
import asyncio from minio import Minio
from reduct import Client as ReductClient CHUNK_SIZE = 100000
CHUNK_COUNT = 10000
BUCKET_NAME = "test" CHUNK = random.randbytes(CHUNK_SIZE) minio_client = Minio("127.0.0.1:9000", access_key="minioadmin", secret_key="minioadmin", secure=False)
reduct_client = ReductClient("http://127.0.0.1:8383") # Our function were here..

if __name__ == "__main__": print(f"Chunk size={CHUNK_SIZE/1000_000} Mb, count={CHUNK_COUNT}") ts = time.time() size = write_to_minio() print(f"Write {size / 1000_000} Mb to Minio: {time.time() - ts} s") ts_read = time.time() size = read_from_minio(ts, time.time()) print(f"Read {size / 1000_000} Mb from Minio: {time.time() - ts_read} s") loop = asyncio.new_event_loop(); ts = time.time() size = loop.run_until_complete(write_to_reduct()) print(f"Write {size / 1000_000} Mb to Reduct Storage: {time.time() - ts} s") ts_read = time.time() size = loop.run_until_complete(read_from_reduct(ts, time.time())) print(f"Read {size / 1000_000} Mb from Reduct Storage: {time.time() - ts_read} s")

    For testings, we need to run the databases. It is easy to do with docker-compose:

    services: reduct-storage: image: reductstorage/engine:v1.0.1 volumes: - ./reduct-data:/data ports: - 8383:8383 minio: image: minio/minio volumes: - ./minio-data:/data command: minio server /data --console-address :9002 ports: - 9000:9000 - 9002:9002

    Run the docker compose configuration and the benchmarks:

    docker-compose up -d
python3 main.py

    Results

    The script print the results for given CHUNK_SIZE and CHUNK_COUNT. On my device, I got the following numbers:

    Chunk Operation Minio Reduct Storage
    10.0 Mb (100 requests) Write 8.69 s 0.53 s
    Read 1.19 s 0.57 s
    1.0 Mb (1000 requests) Write 12.66 s 1.30 s
    Read 2.04 s 1.38 s
    .1 Mb (10000 requests) Write 61.86 s 13.73 s
    Read 9.39 s 15.02 s

    As you can see, Reduct Storage is always faster for write operations (16 times for 10 Mb blobs!!!) and a bit slower for reading when we have many small objects. You may notice that the speed decreasing for both databases when we reduce the size of chunks. This can be explained with HTTP overhead because we spend a dedicated HTTP request for each write or read operation.

    Conclusions

    Reduct Storage could be a good option for applications where you should store blobs historically with timestamps and write data all the time. It has a strong FIFO quota to avoid problems with disk space, and it is very fast for intensive write operations.

    References:

    It is a month-long celebration of open source. For a lot of devs, its their introduction to open source.

    Check out the Hacktoberfest tag on DEV to keep up with the latest!

    Umair Mehmood (TheFatBoy) - Oct 14

    Once suspended, reduct-storage will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, reduct-storage will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by reduct-storage will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If reduct-storage is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag reduct-storage:

    reduct-storage consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging reduct-storage will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: atimin

item.title: Telescope 如何让我成为更好的开发人员 | How Telescope made me a better Developer
item.description:
如果您已经了解我，您可以跳过此部分
首先，我是软件开发学位的学生。 我将在另外两个学期毕业。 我目前在我的合作社的第二个学期。 在 2021-2022 年冬季，我参加了一门名为“开源项目”的课程，我们所有的学生都参与了这个单一的开源项目——望远镜。 本项目使用 React、docker、docusaurus、supabase 等工具。 最后——这取决于我们，学生们把它带到我们想要发展的方向。

那么这门课有什么特别之处呢？

团队规模

首先，它是在单个项目上工作的人数。 那个学期——我们有 17 个人。 到目前为止，我们曾经拥有的最大的团体是 4 人。 我们之前有更长的项目，我们在同一个小组内做了几个学期的计划和实施。 然而，它从来没有那么大。

这模拟了更真实的工作环境。 目前，在我的合作社中，我是一个约 20 人团队的一员，每个人都有自己的角色，但都使用一个工具。 我们都相互交流，寻求帮助、信息、更新、安排会议并相互了解。

现有代码库

到目前为止，在我们的大学作业中，即使那些是更大的项目，甚至在我们的个人项目中——我们总是不得不重新开始。 无论我们的任务是什么，我们都是从 0 开始创建的，而且通常我们对自己的代码和工具非常熟悉。

有了望远镜——那是完全不同的。 我们已经有了上一堂课交给我们的工作应用程序。 我们有很多以前从未见过的代码、工具和决策，我们必须适应这种环境。 我们还遇到了许多他们的老问题、错误和未探索的方向。

这再次很好地模拟了现实的工作环境。 您将是团队中的新人，而他们都取得了很大进步。 您将看到他们的代码并且必须理解它，并对其进行更改以添加您自己的改进。 学习融入现有环境是一项非常有用的发展技能。

探索自由

上大学——你没有太多的自由。 大多数时候——你有一个特定的任务，有具体的说明和具体的工具来实现它。 我们在其他班级确实有一些自由……当然。 在我们更大的项目中，我们必须决定要开发什么。 然而，时间和知识的限制通常会导致我们选择一条熟悉的路径。 如果我们想提供一个等级的产品 - 最后我们最好坚持使用我们可以依赖的工具。

然而，望远镜接受了探索。 不仅我们没有明确的任务——我们是决定任务内容的人。 我们必须选择个人的工作，甚至可以提出我们分配给其他人的任务。 不仅是任务，还有工具。 想要添加一个全新的框架？ 使用新语言？ 没问题。 让它发挥作用取决于你，但它是被允许和支持的——我们，学生，喜欢玩新玩具。 如果您自己对使用这种闪亮的新语言感到兴奋 - 其他人会很高兴为您提供帮助。

管理

现在，我最喜欢的部分。
在大学里，我们永远无法成为老板。 您通常会交付给您的教授。 甚至在你的合作社或一些自由职业者中——你通常是新来者，菜鸟，食物链的最低层。 你永远不是做决定的人。

我们必须领导每周的会议，并对那一周的进展负责。 你必须知道发生了什么。 你必须确保人们做好他们的工作。 你是那个说 - “不，让我们专注于这个而不是那个”。 这太棒了。

现在，不要误会我的意思，那太可怕了。 我——一个什么都不知道的学生怎么会领导其他人？ 我刚到这里，伙计们。 但是，让我解释一下它到底是如何以及为什么很棒的。 或者，至少 - 有用。

开发 VS 经理

我，和我们这里的大多数人一样，一直在想——“管理？谁愿意这样做……与从事这项工作的人交谈有什么乐趣？成为工作的人，实际上是编码，这要酷得多。 " 直到……我试图成为望远镜中的一员。 我意识到——不知何故，我想知道发生了什么。 我想知道我们的进展如何。 我想优先考虑重要的方向。

首先是因为提到的所有这些事情......都是错误的。 我看到一个文档问题，我想修复它。 其他人都没有！ 必须是我。 要解决这些问题，您需要知道发生了什么……所以，好的。 我得弄清楚发生了什么事。 我需要询问人们，阅读代码，与他们交谈。 因为没有人会！ 当我修复所有这些烦人的小步骤时，我意识到我实际上是在做……管理。 因为我正在学习每个人都在做什么，只获得足够的信息来意识到，但还不够深入到实际编码 - 我开始意识到一切。

在望远镜中，人们通常会进入自己的领域。 他们将探索一种新工具，或开发一种新服务，并成为他们自己小王国的“国王”。 现在，突然之间，从技术上讲，我们有了一个大型项目——但实际上——我们有许多新建立的王国分散在各处。 除了他们的国王，没有人知道这些王国是什么。 如果没有人了解整个情况，那么任何人如何才能确定前进的方向？ 人们需要以某种方式进行交流。 我是从他们那里得到这些信息的人。

一旦我知道了信息——我就可以通知王国的其他人。 然后 - 我们可以做出决定。

我不能完全解释为什么这很酷。 但我一直被迫建立沟通途径，软化开发过程的边缘并填补未知的空白。 如果不酷——它肯定很重要。 它必须完成。

它如何提高您作为开发人员的能力

我的兴奋很可能没有转移到你身上。 我明白了——计划、会议、文档、与人交谈……——并不好玩。 但让我告诉你，拥有这样的经历对你有什么帮助。

在工作环境中，您可能会有一名经理。 您将有某种会议，其他成员之间会进行某种交流。

如果您自己曾经担任过该角色-您必须知道正在发生的事情，而您却一无所知。 在这里你有一个最后期限，你有责任让这个齿轮团队
交付产品——你怎么知道你做得好还是被搞砸了？ 现在，突然之间，您需要知道发生了什么。 你需要齿轮来向你传达他们的进展、方向和问题。 你需要开会。

作为开发人员，很容易对管理感到恼火。 有时因为管理层自己实际上很糟糕。 但有时——你真的要了解对方。 一旦您了解他们想从您那里听到什么； 一旦你体验过那个提出问题并得到不清楚答案的人 - 你就会开始改变你自己沟通的方式。

让我们做一个网络开发类比，小！

您有 2 个应用程序端点：前端和后端 API。 您需要 API 调用在它们之间传输信息。

您可以将所有内容转储到 JSON 中并忘记它。 你可以只发送一个东西。 您可以每周、每天或每秒发出一次请求。 什么，以什么格式发送，你多久发送一次？ 您需要知道 API 期望什么。 你需要知道他们的数据格式。 您首先需要知道为什么 API 甚至会要求您提供数据。 你是前端，管理者是 API。 或者 - 反过来，如果您更愿意成为后端。 无论在哪个环境中——您都需要知道双方都在发送和期待什么。 沟通就是这样运作的。

沟通确实是必不可少的。 只要你有一个团队 - 你需要它。 在你职业生涯的某个地方，你可能想晋升到更高的职位。 这些通常涉及到更多的管理，你去的越高。 资深开发者。 系统架构师。 一个团队的领导者。 更高的知识，更高的责任，更高的薪水。 如果你计划成长——这就是道路。 它需要学习这些管理、计划和沟通的技能。 向员工展示您已准备好加薪的更好方法 - 证明您已经具备这些技能。 向他们展示你符合要求。 他们会注意到的。 因为其他开发人员 - 不会。

这就是担任经理职位的经验如何提高您作为开发人员的能力。



原文:

If you already know about me, you can skip this section
Firstly, I am a student of a Software Development degree. I will graduate in 2 more semesters. I am currently in my 2nd semester of co-op. In Winter 2021-2022 I have taken a course called "Open source Project", which involved all of us students working on this single open source project - Telescope. This project uses React, docker, docusaurus, supabase, and other tools. In the end - it was up to us, students to take it in a direction we want to evolve it in.

So what was so special about that class?

Team size

Firstly, it was the amount of people working on a single project. That semester - it was 17 of us. So far the biggest groups we ever had were of 4 people. We had longer projects before, where we did planning and implementation over several semesters within the same group. However, it was never that big before.

This emulates a more realistic work environment. Currently, in my co-op, I am a part of a ~20 person team, all have their roles, but all work on a single tool. We all communicate between each other, ask for help, information, updates, arrange meetings and get to know each other.

Existing codebase

So far in our college assignments, even if those were larger projects, and even in our personal projects - we always had to start anew. No matter what our task was, we create it from 0, and usually we get pretty familiar with our own code and tools.

With Telescope - that was way different. We already had a working application that was handed onto us by the previous class. We had a lot of code, tools and decisions that we have never seen before, and we had to adjust to that environment. We also had many of their older problems, bugs and unexplored directions.

That, again, simulates a realistic work environment well. You will be new in your team, while they all have made a lot of progress. You will see their code and will have to understand it, and change it to add your own improvements. Learning to integrate into an existing environmnent is a very useful skill to develop.

Freedom of exploration

With college - you don't get much freedom. Most of the time - you have a specific task, with specific instructions and concrete tools to implement it. We did have some freedom in our other classes... sure. In our larger project, we got to decide what to develop. However, time and knowledge constraints usually led us to choose a familiar path. If we want to deliver a product for a grade - in the end we better stick with a tool we can rely on.

Telescope, however, embraced exploration. Not only we didn't have a definite task - we were the ones to decide what the tasks will be. We got to choose what to work on personally, and could even come up with tasks we would assign to someone else. It's not only the tasks, but also the tools. Want to add a completely new framework? Use a new language? No problem. It's on you to make it work, but it is allowed and is supported - us, students, like new toys to play with. If you yourself get excited with using this shiny new language - others will get excited to help you.

Management

Now, my favorite part.
In college, we never get to be the boss. You usually deliver to your professor. And even on your co-op, or some freelance work - you are usually the newcomer, the noob, the lowest of the foodchain. You are never the one to make decisions.

Telescope made us temporary managers. We had to lead weekly meetings, and were responsible for that week's progress. You had to know what was going on. You had to make sure people do their jobs. You were the one to say - "no, let's focus on this and not that". And it was awesome.

Now, don't get me wrong, it was terrifying. How - me - a student that knows nothing will lead other people? I just got here, guys. However, let me explain how and why exactly it was awesome. Or, at the very least - useful.

Dev VS Manager

I, as most of us here, have always thought - "Management? Who wants to do that... What fun is it to talk to people who do the work? It's much cooler to be the one doing the work, actually coding." Until.. I tried to be one in Telescope. And I realised - somehow, I want to be aware what's going on. I want to know what our progress is. I want to prioritise directions that are essential.

Firstly it was because all of those things mentioned... WERE WRONG. I see a documentation problem, and I WANT TO FIX IT. And NOBODY ELSE DOES! It has to be me. To fix those things, you need to know what's going on... so, ok. I gotta find out what's going on. I need to ask people, read code, talk to them. BECAUSE NOBODY WILL! And as I'm fixing all these annoying little steps, I realise I'm actually doing.. management. Since I am learning what everyone is doing, getting just enough information to be aware, but not deep enough to actually code - I get to be aware of everything.

In Telescope usually people would go into their own niches. They will explore a new tool, or develop a new service and will become "kings" in their own little kingdom. Now, suddenly, we have technically a large project - but in reality - we have many newly established kingdoms scattered all around. And nobody else knows what those kingdoms are, except their kings. How would anyone figure out the direction to go, if nobody is aware of the whole picture? People need to communicte somehow. And I was the one to get this information out of them.

Once I know the information - I can inform the rest of the kingdom. And then - we can make our decisions.

I can't exactly put my finger on why this is cool. But I keep getting compelled to establish paths of communication, softening the edges of the development process and filling in the gaps of unknowns. If not cool - it for sure is important. It has to be done.

How it improves you as a developer

It's very likely that my excitement didn't transfer onto you. I get it - planning, meetings, documentation, talking to people... - isn't fun. But let me tell you how it helps you to have such an experience.

In the work environment you will likely have a manager. You will have some kind of meetings, some kind of communication between other members.

If you ever have been in that role yourself - where you had to know what is happening, and you just had no idea. Here you have a deadline, and you're responsible that this team of cogwheels
deliver the product - how do you even figure out if you're doing well or are fucked? Now, suddenly, you need to know what is going on. You need cogwheels to communicate to you their progress, direction, problems. You need meetings.

As a developer it is easy to get annoyed with management. Sometimes because the management themselves actually suck. But sometimes - you really gotta understand the other side. Once you understand what they want to hear from you; once you've had experience of the one who's asking and getting unclear answers - you start to change the way you communicate yourself.

Let's make a web dev analogy, wee!

You have 2 application endpoints: front end and back end API. You need API calls to transfer information between them.

You can just dump everything in a JSON and forget about it. You can send just a single thing. You can make a request once a week, once a day, or every second. What, in what format, and how often do you actually send it? You need to know what the API is expecting. You need to know their data format. You need to know why API even asks you for data in the first place. You are the front end, and the manager is the API. Or - the other way around, if you'd prefer to be the back end. No matter in which environment - you need to know what both sides are sending and expecting. That is how communication works.

Communication is, truly, essential. As long as you have a team - you need it. Somewhere in your career you might want to advance onto higher positions. Those usually involve more management the higher you go. A senior developer. A system architect. A team lead. Higher knowledge, higher responsibility, higher salary. If you plan to grow - this is the path. And it requires to learn these skills of management, planning and communication. The better way to show your employees you are ready for a raise - prove you already have these skills. Show them you meet the requirements. And they will notice. Because other developers - won't.

And that is how the experience of being in a manager position improves you as a developer.
item.author: sirinoks

item.title: 在 C 中使用带有 Vely 框架的 cookie | Using cookies in C with Vely framework
item.description:
该数据可以或多或少具有持久性，其程度可以通过 cookie 本身指定。 因此，cookie 可以持续到客户端会话结束，持续一天、一个月或几乎永远。

当然，最终用户可以随时删除 cookie。

在典型的使用场景中，服务器要求浏览器保存一个 cookie，浏览器会这样做，并且它会在以后的每个请求中提供这个值：

设置一个cookie

您可以在服务器响应中设置 cookie。 因此，当客户端向服务器请求页面时，服务器将在 HTTP 响应中发送一个 Set-Cookie 标头。 此标头（除其他外）说明 cookie 名称及其值。 客户端读取并设置 cookie。 这就是它的不足之处。

得到一个饼干

每次客户端发出请求时，它都会发送与其发送请求的服务器相关的所有 cookie。 请记住，这些是服务器在此之前发送给客户端的相同 cookie。

使用 cookie

时间线一般是这样的：

  1. 服务器将回复页面发送回客户端，以及它希望客户端保存的 cookie，

  2. 客户端现在从服务器请求页面，并且每次这样做时，它还会将 cookie 发送回服务器。

举例来说，这可能会更清楚，例如会话身份验证：

  1. 客户端将用户名和密码发送到服务器（即认证请求），

  2. 服务器验证这些，并发送回一个页面，通知客户端已登录，并将会话 ID 作为 cookie，

  3. 客户端现在通过每个后续请求将此 cookie 发送回服务器，并且由于此 cookie 是一个会话 ID，它会向服务器验证自己的身份。

这只是 cookie 的一种可能用途。 但是您明白了它的要点：cookie 包含一些有助于跟踪其状态或应用程序需要跟踪的任何信息的信息。 在这种情况下，它是身份验证。 它可以是一个购物车。 它可以跟踪用户正在阅读哪些文章。 这么多东西。

什么是维利

从内容管理系统到业务应用程序再到交互式网站，一切都可以用 Vely 制作。

使用 Vely 编程意味着您编写声明性语句，并为其生成高度优化的 C 代码 - 最终结果是本机性能和低占用空间。

韦利的饼干

要设置 cookie ，您可以使用 set-cookie 语句：

 set-cookie <cookie name>=<cookie value> \ [ expires <expiration> ] \ [ path <path> ] \ [ same-site "Lax"|"Strict"|"None" ] \ [ no-http-only ] \ [ secure ]

所以最简单的形式就是：

 set-cookie “my_cookie” = “some value”

当您编写此代码时，Web 浏览器会将“一些值”存储为名为“my_cookie”的 cookie。

然后您可以指定所有其他 cookie 属性，例如到期日期（“expires”）、路径（“path”），无论是仅用于 HTTP 还是客户端脚本可访问（“no-http-only”） ，cookie 是否仅适用于同一站点（“同一站点”），以及它是否仅适用于安全 (https) 连接（“安全”）。

所以一个更复杂的例子：

 get-time to define cookie_exp_time month 3 char *my_cookie_val="some cookie value"; set-cookie "tasty_cookie"=my_cookie_val expires cookie_exp_time path "/" same-site "Lax"

在此示例中，您首先获得到期日期，即从现在起 3 个月。 这就是cookie过期的时候。 它的值是从字符串中获取的，并且还指定了路径和同站点属性。 最好从一开始就指定路径，这样以后就不会混淆。

要从调用您的服务器端代码的客户端获取 cookie ：

 get-cookie [ define ] <cookie value> = <cookie name>

例如，要获得上面相同的 cookie 集：

 get-cookie define cookie_val = “tasty_cookie”

字符串变量 cookie_val 是使用“define”子句创建的，它的值将是“一些 cookie 值”。

最后，您可以删除 cookie，这意味着浏览器将删除它，并且它不会在以后的请求中返回：

 delete-cookie <cookie name> \ [ path <cookie path> ] \ [ status [ define ] <status> ]

要在此处删除 cookie ：

 delete-cookie “tasty_cookie” path “/”

为确保您所指的 cookie 始终是正确的，最好始终使用路径。

一个简单的例子将使 cookie 的主题更容易理解。

在这种情况下，浏览器中会显示一个表单，用户可以输入一个名称。 提交表单后，该名称现在被记录为浏览器中的 cookie。

然后，另一个 Web 请求将获取此 cookie 并显示它。

输入名称的 HTML 表单如下所示：

您的 Vely 代码的回复将是：

稍后获取 cookie 值将类似于：

好消息是这个例子是 Vely 安装的一部分。 安装 Vely 后，您无需编写任何代码，只需按照说明获取示例、制作应用程序并运行它即可。

请注意，Vely 是使用标准 Linux 工具安装的，例如 apt、dnf、zypper 或 pacman。 因此，您可以管理 Vely 安装，也可以轻松地将其从系统中删除。



原文:

Cookies are used to keep data on the web client side. This data can be more or less persistent, the degree of which can be specified with the cookie itself. So a cookie could last until the client session ends, for a day, a month, or virtually forever.

Of course, end user can always delete cookies.

A cookie can be used to track what the end-user is doing, to keep the session information, to remember preferences and a myriad of other things.

In a typical usage scenarios, the server asks the browser to save a cookie, and the browser will do so, and also it will provide this value with every future request:

Set a cookie

You can set a cookie in a server response. So, when a client asks for a page from the server, the server will send a Set-Cookie header in the HTTP response. This header (among other things) states the cookie name and its value. A client reads this and sets the cookie. That’s the short of it.

Get a cookie

Each time a client makes a request, it sends all the cookies related to the server it is sending a request to. Keep in mind, these are the same cookies that server sent to the client prior to this.

Using cookies

The timeline generally goes like this:

  1. the web client (i.e. browser) sends a request to server,

  2. the server sends a reply page back to the client, and along with it, the cookie(s) it expects the client to save,

  3. the client now requests pages from the server and each time it does that, it also sends cookies back to the server.

This may be clearer with an example, such as session authentication:

  1. the client sends the user name and password to the server (i.e. a request to authenticate),

  2. the server verifies those, and sends back a page announcing that the client has logged in, and with it, the session ID as a cookie,

  3. the client now sends this cookie back to server with each following request, and since this cookie is a session ID, it authenticates itself to the server.

This is just one possible use of cookies. But you get the gist of it: a cookie contains some information that helps keep track of its state, or of whatever it is that application needs to keep track of. In this case it’s authentication. It can be a shopping cart. It can be tracking which articles is the user reading. So lots of stuff.

What is Vely

Vely is a framework for C programming language that is especially well suited for web applications. Everything from content management systems to business applications to interactive web sites can be made with Vely.

Programming with Vely means you write declarative statements for which highly optimized C code is generated - the end result is native performance and low footprint.

Cookies in Vely

To set a cookie you would use set-cookie statement:

set-cookie <cookie name>=<cookie value> \
    [ expires <expiration> ] \
    [ path <path> ] \
    [ same-site "Lax"|"Strict"|"None" ] \
    [ no-http-only ] \
    [ secure ]

So the simplest form is just:

set-cookie “my_cookie” = “some value”

When you write this, the web browser will store “some value” as a cookie named “my_cookie”.

Then you can specify all the other cookie attributes, such as the expiration date ("expires"), path ("path"), whether it’s just for HTTP or if accessible to client-side scripts ("no-http-only"), whether the cookie applies just to the same site or not ("same-site"), and whether it’s only working with secure (https) connection ("secure").

So a more involved example:

get-time to define cookie_exp_time month 3

char *my_cookie_val="some cookie value";

set-cookie "tasty_cookie"=my_cookie_val expires cookie_exp_time path "/" same-site "Lax"

In this example, you first get the expiration date, which is 3 months from now. So that’s when the cookie will expire. Its value is obtained from a string, and the path and same-site attributes are specified too. It’s good to specify a path from the beginning so there’s no confusion later.

To get a cookie from a client that called your server-side code:

get-cookie [ define ] <cookie value> = <cookie name>

For instance, to get the same cookie set above:

get-cookie define cookie_val = “tasty_cookie”

The string variable cookie_val is created with "define" clause and its value will be “some cookie value”.

Finally, you can delete a cookie, meaning the browser will delete it, and it won’t come back in the future requests:

delete-cookie <cookie name> \
     [ path <cookie path> ] \
     [ status [ define ] <status> ]

To delete the cookie here:

delete-cookie “tasty_cookie” path “/”

To be sure the cookie you’re referring to is always the right one, it’s good to always use the path.

Vely cookie example

A simple example will make the subject of cookies easier to understand.

In this case, a form is displayed in the browser, and the user can enter a name. Upon submitting the form, this name is now recorded as a cookie in the browser.

Then, another web request will obtain this cookie and display it.

The HTML form to enter the name will look like:

The reply from your Vely code will be:

And getting the cookie value later will be like:

The Vely cookie example is here - it contains instructions to a working application:

https://vely.dev/example_cookies.html

The nice thing is that this example is a part of Vely installation. Once you install Vely, you won’t need to write any code, just follow instructions to get the example, make the application and run it.

Note that Vely is installed with standard Linux tools like apt, dnf, zypper or pacman. So you can manage Vely installation, and you can easily remove it from your system as well.
item.author: velydev

item.title: 行情应用 | Quotes App
item.description:
我使用 Angular、RxJS、TypeScript 和 Quotable API 开发了一个 Quotes 应用程序。 随意从中学习和/或提供反馈。 谢谢！

特征

  • 主页上的随机报价，可以刷新以获得另一个。
  • 按作者和标签搜索引用。
  • 单击作者或标签将搜索带有该作者或标签的引号。
  • 查看有关作者的更多信息。

入门

  1. 克隆 repo： git clone https://github.com/nightwolfdev/quotes.git
  2. 导航到项目文件夹： cd quotes
  3. 安装依赖： npm install
  4. 运行应用程序： ng serve
  5. 在浏览器中访问应用程序： http://localhost:4200

链接

  • 演示
  • 角
  • 打字稿
  • 可引用的 API

钱德拉-彭农达 - 一旦暂停，nightwolfdev 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，nightwolfdev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，所有由 nightwolfdev 发布的帖子都将被隐藏并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 nightwolfdev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 nightwolfdev 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 nightwolfdev 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I developed a Quotes application using Angular, RxJS, TypeScript, and the Quotable API. Feel free to learn from it and/or provide feedback. Thanks!

Features

  • Random quote on home page which can be refreshed to get another.
  • Search quotes by author and tag.
  • Clicking an author or tag will search for quotes with that author or tag.
  • View more information about an author.

Getting Started

  1. Clone the repo: git clone https://github.com/nightwolfdev/quotes.git
  2. Navigate to the project folder: cd quotes
  3. Install dependencies: npm install
  4. Run the app: ng serve
  5. Visit the app in a browser: http://localhost:4200

Links

  • Demo
  • GitHub Repo
  • Angular
  • RxJS
  • TypeScript
  • Quotable API

chandra-penugonda - Sep 26

Once suspended, nightwolfdev will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, nightwolfdev will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by nightwolfdev will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If nightwolfdev is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag nightwolfdev:

nightwolfdev consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging nightwolfdev will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 为什么 Discord 对开发者下注很大 | Why Discord is Betting Big on Devs
item.description:
一个拥有 1.5 亿月活跃用户的平台如何为未来打造？ 一句话：开发者。

在本周的 Dev Interrupted 节目中，Discord 平台生态系统副总裁 Anjney Midha 与我们坐下来讨论 Discord 的过去、现在和未来。

在这个令人难以置信的情节中，Anjney 详细介绍了 Discord 最近的产品发布、它与游戏的精神联系，以及为什么一个完全可修改的平台是用户体验的关键。

剧集亮点包括：

  • 当您在这里时，请从我们的YouTube 频道观看此视频，并确保喜欢并订阅！

    想知道 Slack 和 Stripe 的工程师如何将他们的开发团队的工作与业务底线联系起来吗？ 或者 Shopify 和 CircleCI 的团队领导如何保持精英周期时间，同时最大限度地减少开发人员倦怠和最大限度地保留？

    这些只是我们将于 10 月 25 日在Interact上讨论的两个主题。

    免费的、虚拟的、社区驱动的工程领导会议， Interact是一个为期一天的活动，有超过 25 位最受尊敬的开发人员参加，他们全部由 Dev Interrupted 社区的数千名工程领导者选出。

    罗曼·巴拉舍维奇—— 一旦暂停，linearb 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，linearb 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有由 linearb 发布的帖子都将被隐藏并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 linearb 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 linearb 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 linearb 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

How does a platform with 150 million monthly active users build for the future? In a word: Developers.

Discord is betting big that the future of their company, their product and their community is in supporting developers - and they're putting their money where their mouth is.

On this week's episode of Dev Interrupted, Anjney Midha, Discord's VP of Platform Ecosystem, sits down with us to discuss Discord's past, present and future.

In this incredible episode, Anjney details Discord's recent product announcement, its spiritual connection to gaming, and why a totally moddable platform is the key to user experience.

Episode Highlights Include:

  • (3:00) Anjney's journey to Discord
  • (9:17) Discords acquisition of Ubiquity6
  • (13:53) How devs can contribute to Discord
  • (22:34) Discord's big announcement
  • (30:02) Ecosystem fund
  • (37:12) Biggest takeaway for devs

While you’re here, check out this video from our YouTube channel, and be sure to like and subscribe when you do!

You’re invited to Interact on October 25th!

Want to know how engineers at Slack and Stripe connect their dev teams’ work to the business bottom line? Or how team leads at Shopify and CircleCI keep elite cycle time while minimizing dev burnout and maximizing retention?

These are just two of the topics we’ll tackle at Interact on October 25th.

A free, virtual, community-driven engineering leadership conference, Interact is a one-day event featuring over 25 of the most respected minds in development, all selected by the thousands of engineering leaders in the Dev Interrupted community.

Register now!

Roman Balashevich - Sep 11

Once unsuspended, linearb will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If linearb is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 逐步参与 Hacktoberfest 活动 | Steps by Steps Contributing Into Hacktoberfest Event
item.description:
在为Hacktoberfest 活动创建评论后，您可以在此处阅读。 在这个故事中，我想逐步分享为Hacktoberfest 活动做出贡献的步骤。 我制作这个故事是因为我的朋友让我写下如何去做。 我希望它也可以对其他人有所帮助。

工作流程

以上是这些故事的工作流程。 如果您已经了解工作流程，则无需阅读所有这些故事。 但是，世界上可能还有人会使用这篇文章。 所以，让我来写吧。

内容

不做更多介绍，让我们进入这个故事的重点。

如果您对应该贡献哪些存储库感到困惑，请从我之前的文章开始。

提示：每当您尝试查找要贡献的项目时，请阅读CONTRIBUTING.md文件。 在这个项目中，业主要求这样的贡献。

因为它对初学者友好，所以让我们使用这个 repo 作为我们的项目。 找到您感兴趣的后，单击分叉按钮。

这不是抄袭行为。 与 GitHub 带来的 Windows OS 中复制的方法相同。

在命令行中复制克隆命令后，运行此命令。

 git clone <your_coppied_command>

它将下载数据。

正如我们在CONTRIBUTION.md中所读到的，我们应该将图片添加到数据集中。 例如，我想在水果中添加芒果，所以我尝试从谷歌查找芒果图片。 另外，我使用Fatkun Batch Downloader自动下载图片集中的图像文件。 更多信息，你可以在这里阅读我之前的文章。

但是，好吧，它不包含好的图像，所以我必须过滤我将使用哪个图像来贡献。

在这种情况下，我已经在水果部分创建了一个芒果目录。 我把所有过滤后的数据放在这里。

使用命令（不带反斜杠# ）将本地缓存保存到 git 中。

 git add . # adding your changed in local git git commit -m <your message> # write a commit message from your added content using this command git push -u origin # push the local change into your GitHub account using this

或者轻松查看下面的文档。

在命令行中。 这意味着将更改推送到 GitHub 时没有错误。

在重定向的页面上，填写你想要的提交； 如果您添加一条消息会更好。

你的 Pull Requests (PR) 应该在等待名单上。

合并的速度取决于回购的所有者； 它可能需要几分钟、几小时、几天，甚至几周，或者永远不会。 如果他们已经接受了您的 PR，它将被移至 Closed 部分，并且您的 PR 将写在那里。

看看你的 Hacktoberfest，他们的团队应该这样算。

好吧，要有耐心，只贡献大量资源。

结论

读完这篇文章，更多的人可以加入这个活动。 因为本次活动不仅是针对开发者的，本次活动的首要目的也是为了创建对所有人免费的开源资源。 感谢阅读，记住这个故事只是一个例子，您可以关注它或查找其他报告。 请记住这一点，不要发送垃圾邮件，并成为更明智的贡献者。 谢谢。 在 GitHub 上与我联系

梦想家911



原文:

After creating a review for the Hacktoberfest event that you can read here. In this story, I would like to share steps by steps for contributing to the Hacktoberfest event. I made this story because my friends asked me to write about how to do it. I hope it can be helpful to others too.

The Workflow

Above is the workflow of these stories. You don't need to read all of these stories if you already understand the workflow. But, there may be in the world, there still ones who will use this article. So, let me write it.

The Content

Without taking more intros, let's work into the main point of this story.

1.Find the accepted project in GitHub. If you confuse about which repos you should contribute, begin with my article before.

Tips: whenever you try to find a project you want to contribute to, read the CONTRIBUTING.md files. In this project, the owner is asking for the contribution of something like this.

Because it's beginner friendly, let's use this repo as our project. After finding one you are interested in, click the fork button.

2.It will bring you to new pages for those who don't understand a fork. It's not a plagiarism act. It's the same method duplicated in Windows OS brought by GitHub.

3.After the fork is completed, you can see that the project owner is your account, with there is a note that the project is forked from the source's repos.

4.Clone the repositories by copying the git command from here.

After copying the clone command in your command line, run this command.

git clone <your_coppied_command>

It will download the data.

5.The clone command is the same as the download command.

6.Here is the crucial steps because your contributions start here. As we read in CONTRIBUTION.md, we should add a picture to a dataset. For example, I would like to add mango to the fruits, so I try to find mango images from google. Also, I use Fatkun Batch Downloader to download image files in the collection of pictures automatically. For more information, you can read my previous article here.

7.Because I am using the fatkun batch downloader, it will download all images based on the mango fruit search. But, well, it does not contain good images, so I have to filter which image I will use to contribute.

8.After that, I move all the filtered data into the project I want to contribute to. In this case, I already created a mango directory in the fruits section. I placed all the filtered data here.

9.After the data is ready, let's contribute using these steps. Save the local cache into git using the command (without backslash #).

git add . # adding your changed in local git
git commit -m <your message> # write a commit message from your added content using this command 
git push -u origin # push the local change into your GitHub account using this

Or easily see the documentation below.

The message Branch 'main' must be set up to track remote branch 'main' from 'origin.' in the command line. It means there is no error when pushing your change to your GitHub.

10.The header message will be written as your previous commit.

11.Finally, push your changes to the origin repo by clicking the contribute button.

On the redirected page, fill in the commit you want; it will be better if you add a message.

Your Pull Requests (PR) should be on the waiting lists.

The speed of merging depends on the owner of the repo; it can take minutes, hours, days, even weeks, or never maybe. If they already accept your PR, it will be moved to the Closed section, and your PR will be written there.

Check out your Hacktoberfest, and their teams should count it like this.

The Hacktoberfest team will check your PR, and it took about seven days to change into Accepted.

Well, be patient and contribute only great resources.

Conclusions

After reading this, more people can join this event. Because this event is not only for the developer, but the primary purpose of this event is also to create open-source resources that are free to everyone. Thanks for reading, and remember this story is just an example you can follow it or find other reports. Just remember this, don't spam, and be a wiser contributor. Thanks. 😇

Connect with me on GitHub

theDreamer911
item.author: thedreamer911

item.title: Ruby on Rails 的友好 ID | Friendly IDs for Ruby on Rails
item.description:
你有像/books/1或/secret_things/10这样的 URL 并希望它们更友好吗？ 另外，希望 ID 是连续的和可猜测的吗？

我喜欢 Stripe 的 URL，例如https://dashboard.stripe.com/test/products/prod_MG5m4q7sKvGto8 。 如果我在任何我知道它属于 Stripe 产品的地方看到 ID 值prod_MG5m4q7sKvGto8 。 条纹客户 ID。 非常友好。

并且 ID 不是连续的。 友好而随意。 我要这个。

我将向您展示我用于友好 ID 的项目，以及如何将它们添加到我的 Rails 脚手架生成器中，以便为每个模型自动启用它们。

 rails generate scaffold Book title description:text

我的 URL 会自动弹出，看起来像/books/book_qYlVPJvDprRabHa0wy1xz3n9 。

快速调整生成的类，它们可能会变得更好， /books/bk_qYlVPJvDprRabHa0wy1xz3n9 。

是的，你也想要这个。

魔术是由 Chris Oliver 的prefixed_ids ruby​​gem 提供的。

唯一的要求是模型的id列是bigint或其他整数。 我没有成功使用带有uuid列的 gem。 但是您不需要 UUID ID，因为您将拥有公开友好的随机 ID 和内部顺序 ID。

试一试

在我们开始编辑您的脚手架生成器之前，让我们在现有模型上进行尝试。

将has_prefix_id :thing添加到您的模型之一，然后在您的应用程序中查看它。

 class Book has_prefix_id :bk

你无聊的 ID 现在看起来非常友好， /books/bk_x912t423 。

一切正常。

将其添加到您的模型生成器

您肯定希望所有未来模型的友好 ID。 也就是说，当您运行时：

 rails g model Book title rails g scaffold Book title

您希望生成的app/models/book.rb包含上面的has_prefix_id :bk链接。

首先，将当前的 Rails model.rb.tt模板复制到您的应用程序中。

如果您使用的是 Jumpstart Pro，则可以跳过此步骤。

 mkdir -p lib/templates/active_record/model curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rails/rails/main/activerecord/lib/rails/generators/active_record/model/templates/model.rb.tt \ -o lib/templates/active_record/model/model.rb.tt

该文件将用于生成您未来的所有模型类文件。

在新创建的model.rb.tt文件中，添加第 3 行：

 <% module_namespacing do -%> class <%= class_name %> < <%= parent_class_name.classify %> has_prefix_id :<%= singular_name %>

如果你要生成一个Book模型，它看起来像：

 class Book has_prefix_id :book end

您可以将:book编辑为:bk 。 选择一个与您的 URL 欣赏客户产生共鸣的缩写。

向他们展示你的关心。



原文:

Do you have URLs like /books/1 or /secret_things/10 and wish that they were friendlier? Also, wish the IDs were sequential and guessable?

I like Stripe's URLs, such as https://dashboard.stripe.com/test/products/prod_MG5m4q7sKvGto8. If I see the ID value prod_MG5m4q7sKvGto8 anywhere I know it belongs to a Stripe Product. cus_MG5sTiccdSlpjw? A Stripe Customer ID. Very friendly.

And the IDs are not sequential. Friendly and random. I want this.

I'm going to show you the project I use for friendly IDs, and how I add them to my Rails scaffolding generators so they are automatically enabled for every model.

rails generate scaffold Book title description:text

And my URLs automatically pop out looking like /books/book_qYlVPJvDprRabHa0wy1xz3n9.

A quick tweak of the generated class and they might become even nicer, /books/bk_qYlVPJvDprRabHa0wy1xz3n9.

Yeah, you want this too.

prefixed_ids gem

The magic is provided by Chris Oliver's prefixed_ids rubygem.

The only requirement is that your models' id column is bigint or some other integer. I was not successful using the gem with uuid columns. But you won't need UUID IDs because you'll have publicly friendly, random IDs, and internally sequential IDs.

Trying it out

Before we go and edit your scaffold generators, let's try it out on an existing model.

Add has_prefix_id :thing to one of your models, and go and view it in your app.

class Book has_prefix_id :bk

Your boring IDs now look gloriously friendly, /books/bk_x912t423.

The gem hijacks the find and to_param methods. Everything just works.

Adding it to your model generator

You definitely want friendly IDs for all your future models. That is, when you run either:

rails g model Book title
rails g scaffold Book title

You want the resulting app/models/book.rb to include the has_prefix_id :bk link from above.

First, copy the current Rails model.rb.tt template into your app.

If you're using Jumpstart Pro, you can skip this step.

mkdir -p lib/templates/active_record/model
curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rails/rails/main/activerecord/lib/rails/generators/active_record/model/templates/model.rb.tt \
  -o lib/templates/active_record/model/model.rb.tt

This file will be used to generate all your future model class files.

In the newly created model.rb.tt file, add the 3rd line:

<% module_namespacing do -%>
class <%= class_name %> < <%= parent_class_name.classify %>
  has_prefix_id :<%= singular_name %>

If you were to generate a Book model, it would look like:

class Book has_prefix_id :book
end

You can edit :book to :bk. Pick an abbreviation that resonates with your URL-appreciating customers.

Show them you care.
item.author: drnic

item.title: 如何在 Shirates 中使用相对选择器 - 第 3 部分 - | How to use Relative Selector in Shirates - Part 3 -
item.description:
波1008

发表于本文介绍如何使用Shirates移动测试自动化工具。

选择器表达式

您可以使用选择器表达式来选择屏幕中的元素。 这是一个简单而强大的表达式（参见如何在 Shirates 中使用选择器表达式）。

相对选择器

一旦您在屏幕上选择了一个元素，您可能希望相对地在该元素周围获取另一个元素。 在这种情况下，您可以使用 Shirates 的相对选择器。

相对选择器（基于 XML）

你可以得到一个元素与XML的层次结构相对应。

相对选择器

相对选择器描述
父元素
子元素
兄弟元素
祖先元素
后代元素
下一个元素
前一个元素
下一个标签元素
前一个标签元素
下一个输入元素
前一个输入元素
下一个图像元素
上一个图像元素
下一个按钮元素
上一个按钮元素
下一个开关元件
前一个开关元件

相对选择器示例

例子描述
<.class1>:child(1) 选择类型为 class1 的第一个元素，然后选择第一个子元素。
<#id1>:next 选择resource-id为“id1”的第一个元素，然后选择下一个元素。
<text1>:previous 选择文本为“text1”的第一个元素，然后选择前一个元素。
<@access1>:next:next 选择可访问性（内容描述或名称）为“access1”的第一个元素，然后选择下一个元素，然后选择下一个元素。
<@access1>:next(2) 这相当于<@access1>:next:next
<text1>:next(.class1) 选择文本为“text1”的第一个元素，然后选择类为“class1”的下一个元素
<text1>:nextInput 选择文本为“text1”的第一个元素，然后选择下一个输入元素。

材料

您可以从 [ https://github.com/wave1008/shirates-samples-selectors ] 获得完整的示例项目。

了解更多信息

请参阅相对选择器（基于 XML）

结论

一旦您在屏幕上选择了一个元素，您可以使用 Shirates 的相对选择器相对地获取该元素周围的另一个元素。

你可以得到一个元素与XML的层次结构相对应。

原文:

wave1008

Posted on Oct 20

This article is an introduction of how to use Shirates, a mobile testing automation tool.

Selector expression

You can use selector expression for selecting an element in the screen. This is simple and powerful expression(See How to use selector expression in Shirates).

Relative selector

Once you selected an element on the screen, you might want to get another element around the element relatively. In this case, you can use Shirates's relative selector.

Relative selector(XML based)

You can get an element relatively along with hierarichical structure of XML.

Relative selectors

relative selector description
:parent parent element
:child child element
:sibling sibling element
:ancestor ancestor element
:descendant descendant element
:next next element
:previous previous element
:nextLabel next label element
:preLabel previous label element
:nextInput next input element
:preInput previous input element
:nextImage next image element
:preImage previous image element
:nextButton next button element
:preButton previous button element
:nextSwitch next switch element
:preSwitch previous switch element

Relative selector examples

example description
<.class1>:child(1) Select the first element that type is class1, then select the first child.
<#id1>:next Select the first element that resource-id is "id1", then select next element.
<text1>:previous Select the first element that text is "text1", then select previous element.
<@access1>:next:next Select the first element that accessibility(content-desc or name) is "access1", then select next element, then select next element.
<@access1>:next(2) This is equivalent to <@access1>:next:next
<text1>:next(.class1) Select the first element that text is "text1", then select next element that class is "class1"
<text1>:nextInput Select the first element that text is "text1", then select next input element.

Material

You can get complete sample project from [https://github.com/wave1008/shirates-samples-selectors].

SelectWithXmlTest 

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Order
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
import shirates.core.driver.commandextension.classIs
import shirates.core.driver.commandextension.restartApp
import shirates.core.driver.commandextension.select
import shirates.core.testcode.UITest class SelectWithXmlTest : UITest() { @Test @Order(10) fun selectWithXml() { scenario { case(1) { condition { it.restartApp() }.expectation { it.select("Network & internet") { select(":next").classIs("android.widget.TextView") select(":next(2)").classIs("android.widget.LinearLayout") select(":next(3)").classIs("android.widget.LinearLayout") select(":next(4)").classIs("android.widget.ImageView") select(":next(5)").classIs("android.widget.RelativeLayout") select(":next(6)").classIs("android.widget.TextView") select(":next(7)").classIs("android.widget.TextView") } } } } } }

For more information

See Relative selector(XML based)

Conclusion

Once you selected an element on the screen, you can get another element around the element relatively using Shirates's relative selector.

You can get an element relatively along with hierarichical　structure of XML.

item.title: 什么是 Mutator 方法？ | What are Mutator Methods?
item.description:
迈克尔维尼

发表于当我们开始学习 javascript 时，我们都会遇到著名的数组方法。 数组方法可以分为三种类型：

  • 突变方法
  • 访问器方法
  • 迭代器方法

我将为每种数组类型方法创建一个帖子，但今天我将解释第一种类型。

突变方法

这个方法的返回是数组本身的变化。

const animals = ['dog', 'cat', 'turtle', 'bird']; animals.sort();

其他一些例子：

该方法改变原始数组并返回新的长度本身，因此我们可以归类为mutator方法。

const animals = ['dog', 'cat', 'turtle']; const count = animals.push('bird'); console.log(count); // output: 4

控制台日志（动物）； 再见！！！

钱德拉-彭农达 - 萨米娜拉赫曼普尔巴 - 一旦暂停，michaelvinidev 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，michaelvinidev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，michaelvinidev 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 michaelvinidev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 michaelvinidev 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 michaelvinidev 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

MichaelVini

Posted on Oct 20

When we started to learn javascript anytime we will meet the famous array methods. Array methods can be separated in three types:

  • Mutator Methods
  • Accessor Methods
  • Iterator Methods

I will create one post for each array type methods, but today I will explain about the first type.

Mutator Methods

Mutator methods besides return a value, they also change original array, such as:

.sort() - sort method organize array in alphabetical order.

The return of this method is the array itself with the changes.

const animals = ['dog', 'cat', 'turtle', 'bird']; animals.sort();

console.log(animals) // output: ['bird', 'cat', 'dog', 'turtle']

Some others examples:

.push() - push method insert elements into the end of an array

This method change the original array and return of new length itself, so we can classify as mutator method.

const animals = ['dog', 'cat', 'turtle']; const count = animals.push('bird'); console.log(count); // output: 4

console.log(animals); // output: ['dog', 'cat', 'turtle', 'bird']

There are a lot of types of mutator methods and if I explain about all methods I think this post will be very big, but that's not my intention, so I will list some important mutator methods that I think you should be study:

  • .unshift()
  • .shift()
  • .pop()
  • .reverse()
  • .splice()
  • .fill()

See you soon!!!

Rajiv Chaulagain - Oct 20

chandra-penugonda - Oct 19

Samina Rahman Purba - Oct 20

Once suspended, michaelvinidev will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

item.title: ⌨️Akko 3068B（一周后） | ⌨️Akko 3068B (After One Week)
item.description:
库蒂斯弗罗斯特

发表于所以，前几天我发了一篇关于我如何获得新键盘的帖子。 嗯，已经一个星期了，我对它的感觉没有改变。

我仍然非常喜欢这个键盘。 打字仍然是一个梦想，我真的很喜欢里面的果冻粉红色开关。 并非一切都像看起来那么好。 我确实对董事会有一些抱怨：

按键有时非常敏感。 这不是电路板或开关本身的错误，而是我自己的人为错误。 一般来说，我不习惯线性开关或机械键盘的感觉。 我将不得不继续使用它，直到我习惯它为止。 不过，从好的方面来说，键帽感觉质量很好，我无法停止对 Jelly Pinks 的滔滔不绝。 我还没有做的一件事是做磁带模型或创可贴模型。 我听说这两件事一起可以使键盘听起来更好。 尤其是在开关正确润滑的情况下。 我还没有任何工具，但是，当我有的时候，我会让这个坏男孩听起来很棒！

没有打印屏幕按钮。 同样，这不是董事会的错。 我买的时候就知道不会有打印屏幕按钮，我对此没意见。 但是，由于我在 Photoshop 中工作时经常使用该按钮，所以我有点想念它。 当然，我可以使用 Akko 软件为其映射一个新按钮，但是，上次我这样做时，它最终将我的 W、S、A 和 D 键换成了相应的箭头键。 不太清楚为什么会这样。 我只需要重置电路板的内部存储器，一切都已修复。 所以一开始这有点烦人，直到我发现要重置内存。 一旦我这样做了，一切都恢复了正常。 从那以后我没有尝试设置宏备份。

除了这两件事，我真的不能抱怨这个板。 正如我多次说过的那样，该板是一种打字和使用的乐趣。 我还没有做任何脚本或任何事情。 它主要是在打字，但我无法想象这种体验有什么不同。 我还想再次指出，我真的没有其他键盘可以比较这个，所以我主要是靠第一印象来运行的。 如果我现在必须给这块板打 5 分，我可能会给它 4.5/5。 这是一个很棒的初学者板，我非常喜欢它。 我想尝试更多的线性开关来尝试，看看是否有一些我比果冻粉更喜欢的。 我着眼于几个不同的，但是，在我开始深入研究之前，我正在尝试了解更多。

好吧，这就是今天的内容。 感谢您的阅读，希望您有美好的一天！

一旦暂停，kurtissfrost 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 kurtissfrost 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Kurtiss Frost

Posted on Oct 20

So, I made a post the other day about how I had gotten a new keyboard. Well, it's been one week and my feelings on it haven't changed.

I am still very much in love with this keyboard. It is still a dream to type on and I really enjoy the Jelly Pink switches that are inside of it. Not everything is as good as it seems though. I do have a few complaints about the board:

The keys are super sensitive at times. This isn't so much a fault of the board or switches themselves as it is my own human error. I am not used to the feel of linear switches or mechanical keyboards in general. I'm just going to have to keeping using it until I get used to it. On the bright side though, the key caps feel quality and I can't stop gushing about the Jelly Pinks. One thing I have still not done yet is doing the tape mod or the band aid mod. I heard that both of those things together can make the keyboard sound WAY better. Especially if the switches are properly lubed. I don't have any of the tools for that just yet but, when I do, I am going to make this bad boy sounds amazing!

No print screen button. Again, this is not a fault of the board. I knew when I bought it that there wasn't going to be a print screen button and I was okay with that. BUT, since I use that button a lot when I'm working in Photoshop, I kinda miss the hell out of it. Sure I can use the Akko software to map a new button for it but, the last time I did this, it wound up swapping my W,S,A,& D key to the corresponding arrow keys. Not really sure why that happened. I just had to reset the board's internal memory and everything was fixed. So that was kind of an annoyance at first until I found out about resetting the memory. Once I did that, everything went back to normal. I haven't tried to set the macros back up since then.

Other than those 2 things, I can't really complain about this board. As I have stated many times, the board is a blast to type on and a joy to use. I haven't gotten to do any scripting or anything with it yet. It's mostly just been typing but, I can't imagine that experience is any different. I would also like to point out again that, I don't really have another keyboard to compare this too so I'm just running primarily off of first impressions. If I had to rate this board right now out of 5, I would probably give it 4.5/5. This is a great beginner board and I enjoy it so much. I want to try to get some more linear switches to try out and see if maybe there are some out there that I like more than the Jelly Pinks. I have my eyes on a few different ones but, I'm trying to learn more before I start diving too deep into it.

Well, that's really about it for the day. Thanks for reading and I hope you have a wonderful day!

item.title: 刷新自己的反应（长时间休息后） | Refreshing myself on react (after a long break)
item.description:
泰勒卡罗尔

发表于自从我接触到反应以来，已经过了很热的一分钟。 我对它的理解会很差，这让我有点紧张。 所以，我想出了一个游戏计划来更新我的反应技能并获得足够好的报酬🤞💰！

回去学习一个令人惊叹的Udemy课程，我在不久前就完成了它的一部分。 关于这门课程，我最喜欢的部分是一个人在完成一两个概念后所做的迷你项目。 我喜欢做的是添加一个课程不需要的功能，这反过来又迫使我在使用迷你项目作为指导的同时一直自己解决问题。

我在 React 中建立了一个名为 GEM 的项目 - 全球地震监测器。 这是迄今为止我最先进的项目，也是我最引以为豪的项目。它在谷歌地图上绘制了过去一个月世界各地的所有地震，并实时更新。 圆圈越大，地震的威力越大。 不同的颜色对应于地震的最近程度，每次地震都是可点击的，因此您可以获得有关它的数据。 震级、时间和地点（震中）等数据，以及人们是否感受到。 不幸的是它坏了，我需要先磨练我的反应技能才能弄清楚如何修复它。

完成上述第二步后，我可以为该项目添加功能或构建其他新的咳嗽咳嗽反应原生咳嗽！

我认为在完成大部分或全部反应课程并修复我的项目并添加功能或构建其他东西之后，我“应该”拥有入门级技能来支付账单。 无论哪种方式，它都会很有趣，我希望你能跟随我成长为一个更好的程序员！

如果您愿意关注我，我会在我取得进展时发布更新。 随意提出任何你会改变或添加到我的游戏计划中的建议。 谢谢阅读！

原文:

Tyler Carroll

Posted on Oct 20

It's been a hot minute since I have touched react. And it's got me a little nervous that my understanding of it will be poor. So, I am coming up with a game plan to both refresh my react skills and get good enough to be paid for them 🤞💰!

*1. Go back through an AMAZING Udemy course I made it part of the way through awhile back. My favorite part about the course are the mini projects that one does after going over a concept or two. What I like to do is add a feature that the course doesn't require which in turn forces me to figure things out by myself all the while using the mini project as a guide.

*2. I built a project in react I called G.E.M -global earthquake monitor. It is by far my most advanced project and the one I take the most pride in. It plots all earthquakes around the world for the past month on a google map, updating in real time. The larger the circle the more powerful the quake. Different colors correspond to how recent a quake is and each quake is clickable so you can get data about it. Data like the magnitude, time and place (epicenter) and was it felt by people. Unfortunately it's broken and I need to first hone my react skills in order to figure out how to fix it.

*3. After step two above is completed I may either add features to that project or build something else new cough cough react native cough!

I figure after going through either most of or all of the react course and fixing my project and adding features or building something else I "should" have entry level skills to pay the bills. Either way it's gonna be fun and I hope you follow along as I grow to be a better programmer!

If you would give me a follow, I'll be posting updates as I make progress. Feel free to give suggestions of anything you would change or add to my game plan. Thanks for reading!

item.title: tão rápido quanto um celeron 🐌 | tão rápido quanto um celeron 🐌
item.description:
法拉，德夫！

丸💊，uma 时事通讯 em cápsulas de conteúdos para devs。

作为 mudanças agora trarão mais 功能，como anotações，desenhos e assinaturas diretamente no browser，trazendo o Firefox como um "concorrente" mais próximo do Adob​​e Acrobat。 作为实施 podem ser lidas nas发行说明做 Firefox🔗 。

奥斯瓦尔多·林哈雷斯 - 迈克尔·塔灵顿 - 一旦暂停，ezcommunity 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

举报其他不当行为

原文:

Fala, Dev!

Estamos de volta com mais uma edição da EZ.pills 💊, uma newsletter em cápsulas de conteúdos para devs.

Você sabia que o primeiro microprocessador do mundo foi criado a mais de 50 anos atrás? 🤔

ANPD quer criar concurso para 213 servidores

Após a Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados ser transformada em autarquia independente pelo Congresso Nacional, houve uma necessidade maior de força de trabalho. A equipe atual conta com 99 pessoas (sendo 19 terceirizados), porém Waldemar Gonçalves, presidente da autoridade, afirma que a extensão do país e quantidade de indivíduos faz necessária um aumento de mão de obra. A notícia pode ser lida no Convergência Digital🔗.

Microsoft melhora experiência do Windows Update no Windows 11

A última build de preview melhora o gerenciamento de atualizações por parte dos administradores de TI e corrige diversos problemas que levavam a aplicativos "crashando". A maioria das mudanças foi feita no Update Stack Orchestrator, deixando inclusive com que administradores escolham manualmente os dias e horários para as atualizações. A notícia pode ser lida no Bleeping Computer🔗.

Pesquisa indica que geração Z não se importa tanto com cybersegurança no trabalho

A empresa EY entrevistou aproximadamente 1000 funcionários para a pesquisa, onde 83% dos entrevistados informaram estarem cientes dos protocolos de segurança. Apesar disso, os funcionários da geração Z se mostraram mais propensos a reciclar senhas, aceitar quaisquer cookies e outras práticas consideradas inseguras, talvez por estarem mais confortáveis com tecnologia. A notícia pode ser lida no Tech Radar🔗.

Firefox 106 dá grande salto em seu visualizador de PDFs

O gerenciador de PDFs do Firefox é baseado em JavaScript e já tem recebido muita atenção desde 2021, quando começou a abrir PDFs de preenchimento de formulário de forma nativa. As mudanças agora trarão mais features, como anotações, desenhos e assinaturas diretamente no browser, trazendo o Firefox como um "concorrente" mais próximo do Adobe Acrobat. As implementações podem ser lidas nas release notes do Firefox🔗.

FreeBSD chega ao Hypervisor lightweight da AWS

O "herói da comunidade AWS" Colin Percival foi o responsável pelo suporte ao SO no Amazon EC². Agora, ele anunciou que o FreeBSD também suportará outra plataforma: o Amazon Firecracker hypervisor, tendo elementos derivados do crosvm baseado em Rust do Google ChromeOS. A notícia pode ser lida no The Register🔗.

E assim nós finalizamos mais uma edição da sua dose semanal de pílulas de conteúdo! 💙

Quer ter acesso à newsletter inteira e com conteúdos bônus? Faça sua inscrição e receba na sua inbox toda quinta-feira!

Oswaldo Linhares - Sep 27

Michael Tharrington - Sep 19

item.title: 用高阶函数整理getServerSideProps(Server-SideProps) | Tidying up getServerSideProps(Server-Side Rendering) with Higher Order Functions
item.description:
我们都去过那里； 一旦我们的项目变得更大， getServerSideProps就开始变得难以管理。 因为我们必须处理缓存、数据获取、身份验证检查和数据格式化以降低客户端的复杂性。
发生这种情况时，会大大降低开发人员体验（DX）； 出于这个原因，开发人员需要四处寻找他们想要的代码。
当您第一次看到一个大的getServerSideProps时，您可能已经听说过类似的内容 - “缓存头在哪里”或“我们在哪里进行身份验证检查”。

现在，您可能会想 - 如果您只是将一些代码提取到有意义的函数中，这些都很容易躲避。
你是对的。 可以这样做，但它们会使您的getServerSideProps非常密集和冗长。

让我们仔细看看这里的代码：

 export async function getServerSideProps ({ req , res }) { const userIsAuthenticated = req . auth . user . id ; if ( ! userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } /* Big logging section */ /* Check if the data available in redis or memcache */ /* If not get data from Db */ /* Format data for the client */ res . setHeader ( ' Cache-Control ' , ' public, s-maxage=10, stale-while-revalidate=59 ' ); return { props : { time : new Date (). toISOString (), }, }; }

我们可以把它变成这样：

 export async function getServerSideProps ({ req , res }) { bounceUsers ( req ); logStuff ( req ); const cacheData = getCacheData ( req ); const dbData = getDataFromDb (); const formatData = formatDataForClient ( cacheData , dbData ); res . setHeader ( ' Cache-Control ' , ' public, s-maxage=10, stale-while-revalidate=59 ' ); return { props : { data : formatData , }, }; }

在这种情况下，我会选择后者。 但总有改进的余地。 我们仍然可以通过将代码分成单独的高阶函数来提高代码的可读性。
因此，函数将只知道它们需要的部分，开发人员不必四处寻找代码，并且能够在眨眼之间找到他们需要的代码块。

让我们将代码重构为更实用的方式：

 type Options = { kickUser : boolean ; }; type InjectedProps = { userLocation : string ; }; type GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps = GetServerSidePropsContext & InjectedProps ; type Callback = ( context : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps , ) => Promise < GetServerSidePropsResult < any >> ;

如果您正在使用 Typescript，您首先需要添加几个类型以使其与 TS 一起使用。 不要担心InjectedProps和Options ，现在，一会儿就会到达那里。

 export async function getServerSideProps ({ req , res }) { const userIsAuthenticated = req . auth . user . id ; if ( ! userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } return { props : { time : new Date (). toISOString (), }, }; }

让我们首先把它变成一个高阶函数。

 export const bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const userIsAuthenticated = ctx . req . auth . user . id ; if ( options ?. kickUser && userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } const getUserLocation = await magicalLocationFinderFunction (); const injectedContext = { ... ctx , userLocation : getUserLocation (), }; return cb ?.( injectedContext ) || {}; }; }; const getServerSideHomeProps = async ( context : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { return { props : { message : ' Me cookie monster. That all there is to it. Me love to eat cookie. Sometimes eat whole, sometimes me chew it. ' , }, }; }; export const getServerSideProps = bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet ( getServerSideHomeProps , { kickUser : false , });

如果您熟悉高阶函数 (HOC) 的概念，那对您来说应该不会那么陌生。 本质上，我们所做的只是用bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet包装我们的getServerSideHomeProps 。

因此，当我们收到请求时，我们将首先检查用户是否以我们想要的方式进行了身份验证； 如果是这样，我们将检查我们传递kickUser的参数。 然后，如果所有条件都满足，我们会让用户进入getServerSideHomeProps并向客户端传递消息。 我们还在上下文中注入了额外的信息来查看用户的位置。 我们可以很容易地用这种方法丰富我们的上下文。

让我们配置缓存头和数据获取。

 type Options = Partial < { kickUser : boolean ; cacheMaxAge : number ; } > ; export const bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const userIsAuthenticated = ctx . req . auth . user . id ; if ( options ?. kickUser && ! userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } const getUserLocation = await magicalLocationFinderFunction (); const injectedContext = { ... ctx , userLocation : getUserLocation (), }; return cb ?.( injectedContext ) || {}; }; }; export const setCacheHeaders = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { ctx . res . setHeader ( ' Cache-Control ' , `public, s-maxage= ${ options ?. cacheMaxAge } , stale-while-revalidate=59` , ); return cb ?.( ctx ) || {}; }; }; export const fetchData = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const formattedData = await getDataFromDbOrCache (); const injectedContext = { ... ctx , formattedData , }; return cb ?.( injectedContext ) || {}; }; }; const getServerSideHomeProps = async ( context : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const formattedData = context . formattedData ; return { props : { message : formattedData ?? ' Me cookie monster. That all there is to it. Me love to eat cookie. Sometimes eat whole, sometimes me chew it. ' , }, }; }; export const getServerSideProps = bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet ( setCacheHeaders ( fetchData ( getServerSideHomeProps ), { cacheMaxAge : 1000 }), { kickUser : false , }, );

如您所见，此模式可以根据您的需要轻松扩展。 而且，通过这种模式，我们可以轻松避开臃肿的getServerSideHomeProps 。

这种模式的优点

  • 可以轻松扩展
  • 原子
  • 比常规getServerSideProps更具声明性
  • 更容易调试
  • 包装器可以很容易地重复使用

这种模式的缺点

  • 乍一看可能很难理解

利大于弊。 但是，如果您的需求不如这些复杂，请不要费心将这种模式实现到每个 SSR 函数中。 不要忘记保持简单。

何塞·巴勃罗·拉米雷斯·巴尔加斯 - 一旦暂停，ogzhanolguncu 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。
